{{Quote|A squadron of TIE fighters? Great! ''Anything's'' better than facing my father …|Mazer Rackus|<ref name="Galaxy Guide 9" />}}
'''Mazer Rackus''', a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]], was a longtime [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilot]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Rackus originally joined the Rebellion at age sixteen, well before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], after stealing his first [[Starship/Legends|starship]] from his [[Mazer Rackus's father|father]] and crashing it on a Rebel-held [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. Rackus convinced his Rebel captors of his loyalty to their cause and went on to fly raids for the Alliance for many [[Standard year/Legends|years]], although he always carried with him a bad recollection of his [[Parent/Legends|father]].

==Biography==
Mazer Rackus was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]]<ref name="Star Wars: Rebellion" /> sometime before [[16 BBY/Legends|16 BBY]].<ref name="Galaxy Guide 9/Rebellion note" /> He stole his first [[Starship/Legends|starship]] from his [[Mazer Rackus's father|father]] at age sixteen and promptly crashed the vessel on a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] controlled by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].<ref name="Galaxy Guide 9" />

The Rebels captured Rackus, and, though he soon convinced them of his "loyalties" to their cause, he later admitted among friends that he was more afraid of facing his [[Parent/Legends|father]] than anything else. Rackus began flying raids for the Alliance as a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilot]] from that point forward.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 9" /> By the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Rackus had been fighting with the Rebellion for many [[Standard year/Legends|years]].<ref name="Rebellion Guide">[[Star Wars: Rebellion: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Rebellion'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|That ten degree list to port? That must be, uh…''mechanical problems''. Yeah. That's it…|Mazer Rackus|<ref name="Galaxy Guide 9" />}}
Mazer Rackus was a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] of typical appearance for his [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="Galaxy Guide 9" /> He had [[Color/Legends|pinkish-white]] skin, red [[Eye/Legends|eyes]],<ref name="Star Wars: Rebellion" /> and long [[Lekku/Legends|lekku]] extending from his [[Bone/Legends|cranium]]. However, rather than the luxurious [[Clothing/Legends|robes]] traditionally worn by Twi'leks, Rackus usually wore a standard [[Flight suit/Legends|flight suit]]. He also had a taste for spending [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|money]].<ref name="Galaxy Guide 9" />

Although loud and brash, Rackus demonstrated unusual sensitivity for a Twi'lek, hating to see anything beautiful damaged. He was also particularly sensitive of his [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] abilities, and nothing ever happened by accident. Rather than admitting to pilot error, for example, Rackus had an excuse waiting to explain his mishap. Rackus adored speed and risk-taking, as long as it did not cause his ship to crash. He was glad to engage in [[Starfighter combat/Legends|dogfights]] with enemy [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] but made sure to avoid [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]]. He did not necessarily fear [[Death/Legends|death]], but he knew capital ships had enough firepower to imperil him and potentially cause another crash, which prompted Rackus to recall wincing thoughts of his father.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 9" />

==Skills and abilities==
Rackus possessed [[Technician/Legends|mechanical ability]] in [[Astrogation/Legends|astrogation]], [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] operation, starfighter piloting, and starship [[Gunner/Legends|gunnery]]. He was technically adept at repairing [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]].<ref name="Galaxy Guide 9" />

==Equipment==
Rackus's equipment included a [[Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc./Legends|Merr-Sonn]] [[Model 44 blaster pistol]] in a shoulder [[Holster/Legends|holster]], [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], [[Macrobinoculars/Legends|macrobinoculars]], [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]], [[Hydrospanner/Legends|hydrospanner]], three [[Medpac/Legends|medpacs]], his Rebel uniform, dark [[starglasses]], and his personal [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing fighter]].<ref name="Galaxy Guide 9" />

==Behind the scenes==
Mazer Rackus was created for the [[1993]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook ''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]'', written by [[Simon Smith]] and [[Eric S. Trautmann]]. He serves as a character example of a Rebel [[operative]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. Artist [[Mike Vilardi]] provided the sourcebook's illustration of Rackus.<ref name="Galaxy Guide 9" />

Rackus later appeared in the [[1998]] [[Star Wars video games|PC game]] [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']] as one of the playable characters available to the Rebel Alliance.<ref name="Star Wars: Rebellion" /> In gameplay, Rackus is used most effectively as a [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|Special Forces]] operative in conjunction with fellow Rebel character [[Ma'w'shiye]], both of whom exhibit excellent combat and [[Spy/Legends|espionage]] skills. When paired, they can succeed in the most difficult missions.<ref name="Rebellion Guide" />

''Galaxy Guide 9'' originally described Rackus as possessing "piercing green eyes."<ref name="Galaxy Guide 9" /> However, the original character illustration created for ''Star Wars: Rebellion'' depicts Rackus as having red eyes.<ref name="Star Wars: Rebellion" />
